title: Hydropark Skill Manifest
description: Phase-1 PRODUCTION contract for a Hydropark skill manifest. Promotes SPECIFICATION.md §8.2 from an illustrative example to a validated contract that both the desktop client (install/load) and the certification pipeline (P1-20) enforce. A skill is a signed, declarative `prompt + tools + UI pack` (§8.1) — it ships NO model weights and NO executable code. THE MANIFEST IS THE AUTHORING ARTIFACT: it carries the FULL `persona.system_prompt` (the paid IP), which is packaged verbatim into the signed `.hpskill` (§8.8). The Catalog API deliberately serves only `persona.compressed_prompt` before purchase and NEVER the full prompt (BACKEND-DESIGN §3.2 / §4.2, fix SF8). Fields marked `shorthand` have a documented canonical expansion the client normalizes on load (§9.2); see contracts/README.md. Structural budgets here (maxItems / maxLength) are the first line of the §8.5 resource budget; token-accurate limits and the certified `cost_estimate` are produced by the behavioral eval suite (P1-20.1 / P1-20.2).

manifest_version.description: Version of the manifest FORMAT (this schema), independent of the skill's own `version`. Lets the loader route future manifest shapes. Omitted → treated as "1.0".

id.description: Stable, globally-unique skill slug (kebab-case). This is the SPEC skill id and the `skills.id` primary key server-side (BACKEND-DESIGN §3.2). Immutable across versions; a new id is a new product.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the marketplace and the composed agent (e.g. "Cooking Assistant").

summary.description: Optional one-line marketplace pitch (§11.1). Longer, localized descriptions live in the `resources.strings` tables, not here.

version.description: The skill's own semantic version (§8.6). Breaking changes MUST bump major; templates pin version constraints against this (§8.6, §10).

status.description: Lifecycle state (§8.6). `published` = sellable/installable; `deprecated` = owned-but-hidden from new purchase; `withdrawn` = no longer offered (already-owned offline copies keep working unless a safety hard-block applies, §13.7).

category.description: Marketplace category. Constrained to the curated first-party taxonomy (§3 / §27.1 everyday-domain beachhead). Arbitrary categories are rejected so the catalog stays coherent.

min_app_version.description: Minimum app (desktop shell) version required to install/enable (§8.6 compatibility gating). The app refuses to enable a skill whose `min_app_version` exceeds it.

requirements.description: Hardware / model gates evaluated at install and enable (§8.6). Distinct from the runtime capacity meter (§8.3.5).

min_model_tier.description: Minimum base-model tier (§8.6). `small` = the reference ~3B model (Qwen2.5-3B); `mid` = an optional larger model (Phase 2). On minimum hardware a `mid` skill shows a "needs a bigger model" badge and never silently degrades.

min_params_b.description: Advisory minimum model size in billions of parameters (SPEC §8.2 example uses 3).

min_ram_gb.description: Advisory minimum system RAM in GB for a usable experience (§7.3 tiers).

pricing.description: Base pricing declaration. This is the AUTHOR-DECLARED reference price only; the authoritative, regional (PPP) and tax-inclusive price is derived server-side per (target, region) at checkout (§26.3, BACKEND-DESIGN §3.2 `regional_prices`). SHORTHAND: `{"free": true}` (no price) or `{"free": false, "price_usd": 5}`. CANONICAL: `{"free": false, "price": {"amount_minor": 500, "currency": "USD"}}` (integer minor units, never floats — BACKEND-DESIGN §3). A paid skill MUST carry exactly one of `price_usd` / `price`; a free skill MUST carry neither.

free.description: True for the onboarding free skills (§26.4); false for a paid $5 (regionalized) skill.

price_usd.description: SHORTHAND price in whole/decimal US dollars (SPEC §8.2 uses `5`). Normalized to `{amount_minor: round(price_usd*100), currency: "USD"}`.

price.description: CANONICAL base price in ISO-4217 minor units.

amount_minor.description: Integer minor units (e.g. 500 = $5.00). Never a float.

currency.description: ISO-4217 code of the reference/base price (regional currencies are resolved server-side).

persona.description: The prompt fragment that steers the base model (§8.1 #1, §8.3.1).

role.description: Whether this skill may lead the agent's voice (§8.3.1). `primary_eligible` may be chosen as primary and contribute its FULL `system_prompt`; `secondary_only` never leads and contributes only its `compressed_prompt`. SHORTHAND: omitted → `primary_eligible`.

system_prompt.description: The FULL persona/system prompt — the paid intellectual property. Present here because the manifest is the authoring artifact and is packaged verbatim into the signed `.hpskill` (§8.8). IT IS NEVER SERVED PRE-PURCHASE: the Catalog API exposes only `compressed_prompt` (BACKEND-DESIGN §3.2 stores `compressed_prompt`, not `system_prompt`; §4.2 fix SF8), and preview runs never feed it live user input (BACKEND-DESIGN §4.2 N1). Used verbatim as the lead voice only when this skill is primary (§8.3.1). Certification enforces the token budget (§8.5, P1-20.1); the `maxLength` here is a coarse structural guard, not the token limit.

compressed_prompt.description: A 1–2 line capability snippet (§8.3.1). This is the ONLY persona text served to unentitled clients (catalog listing/detail, BACKEND-DESIGN §4.2) AND the text every SECONDARY skill contributes to a composed agent (§8.3.2 "You also have these specialties:" block). Must summarize capability without reproducing the paid prompt.

few_shot.description: Optional few-shot examples steering the model (§8.1 #1). Counted against the prompt-token budget (§8.5).

capabilities.description: The tool CATEGORIES this skill uses, surfaced to the user at install as a plain-language summary (§8.5, §11 "This skill can: set timers, convert units, manage a list"). Constrained to first-party tool categories. NO network / filesystem / system capabilities exist for skills in v1 (§8.5) — the closed enum is the enforcement: any such value fails validation. Should be consistent with the `tools` list (certification cross-checks; P1-20.1).

tools.description: Declarative references to first-party tools the model may call (§8.1 #2). Skills NEVER ship tool implementations — `ref` must name a tool in the fixed, audited catalog (§8.5); the implementation lives in the app's Tool Runtime (P1-05.1). Requesting a new tool means proposing a reviewed, versioned catalog addition, not shipping code.

shared_state.description: Named shared-state slots this skill reads/writes so combined skills can share data (§8.1 #4, §8.3.4). The per-agent shared store hosts these; widgets bind to them via `binds_state` and tools read/write them via `reads_state`/`writes_state`. Two skills declaring the same slot must be structurally compatible after optional-field widening or the combination is blocked (§8.3.4, P1-04.5).

ui.description: The declarative UI pack (§8.1 #3): panels built ONLY from the fixed widget library (§9). Skills never ship HTML/JS/CSS (§9.1).

panels.description: Panels to render, each a widget declaration (§9.2). Manifest panels may use SHORTHAND (inline type-specific props, omitted region/priority/emits/min_widget_version); the app normalizes to the canonical widget contract on load (§9.2).

resources.description: Optional bundled resources (§8.1 #6). v1 skills are prompt-only (no RAG). The ONLY skill-supplied visuals are vetted, sanitized SVG icons and localized string tables (§9.1); no code, no remote content, no inline styles.

icon.description: Primary skill icon — a package-relative sanitized SVG path (must also appear in `assets`).

assets.description: Package-relative asset paths. SANITIZED SVG ONLY (§9.1): the pattern forbids remote URLs and non-.svg files; certification (P1-03.4 / P1-20.1) additionally strips scripts, event handlers, external references and inline styles.

strings.description: Declared locale codes for which a localized string table ships (§8.7). Certification verifies completeness (P1-20.3); a missing locale falls back to `localization.default_locale`.

default_locale.description: Fallback locale when a declared/requested locale is missing (§8.7). SHOULD be present in `resources.strings` (certification cross-checks; P1-20.3).

unit_system_default.description: Default measurement system (§8.7). `auto` follows OS/region. SHORTHAND: omitted → `auto`. Unit-bearing tools/widgets re-render all quantities on change, in chat and panels and shared-state.

conflicts_with.description: Skill ids that cannot be enabled alongside this one (§8.3.6). The UI blocks the combination and explains why (e.g. two skills that both demand primary voice for safety-critical output).

combine_priority.description: Deterministic merge weight (§8.3.2). Higher wins: default primary is the highest-priority `primary_eligible` skill; merge order is primary → `combine_priority` desc → `id` asc, governing prompt assembly, tool-list order, panel stacking, and writer-of-record arbitration (§8.3.4). SHORTHAND: omitted → 50.

cost_estimate.description: SELF-DECLARED resource cost, feeding the capacity meter (§8.3.5). NOTE: the capacity meter trusts the CERTIFICATION-MEASURED figure from the behavioral eval suite (P1-20.2), not this self-declared value alone (§8.3.5); this field is the author's estimate and a certification input, and is budget-checked (P1-20.1).

prompt_tokens.description: Estimated tokens contributed by this skill's prompt (full if primary, compressed if secondary) + tool schemas + few-shot.

signature.description: Detached package signature over the `.hpskill` archive (§8.8). Ed25519 (skill-package key class, distinct from the license-signing key). POPULATED BY THE BACKEND REGISTRY AT PACKAGING TIME — NOT authored by the skill author; absent in a source/authoring manifest. The app verifies it offline via the embedded public key before install and rejects tampered/unsigned packages.

signing_key_id.description: Identifier of the package-signing key that produced `signature` (BACKEND-DESIGN §3.2 `skill_versions.signing_key_id`). Registry-populated.

semver.description: Semantic version `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` with optional pre-release/build metadata.

skillId.description: Kebab-case slug: lowercase alphanumerics separated by single hyphens.

slotName.description: Shared-state slot / list id: snake_case identifier.

widgetId.description: Panel/widget id, unique within the agent (§9.2). snake_case identifier.

localeCode.description: BCP-47-style locale: a two-letter language code with an optional region (e.g. `en`, `uk`, `en-US`).

widgetVersion.description: Widget-library version constraint the panel requires (§9.8), e.g. `1.0`.

svgAssetPath.description: Package-relative sanitized-SVG path. Forbids remote URLs (no `://`), path traversal, and any non-.svg extension (§9.1).

toolRef.description: A reference into the FIXED, first-party, audited tool catalog (§8.1 / §8.5). This closed enum IS the safety backbone: skills cannot name a tool outside it, and adding a tool means a reviewed, versioned catalog change (P1-05.1) — not shipping code.

capability.description: A first-party tool CATEGORY (§8.5). Deliberately excludes network/filesystem/system — no such capability exists for v1 skills.

widgetType.description: A widget type from the v1 core widget library (§9.4). `chart`/`sparkline`/`map` are deferred to Phase 2 and are intentionally absent.

slotSchema.description: A shared-state slot type in the CLOSED type language (§8.3.4): `scalar<string|number|bool|enum(A|B|...)>`, `list<T>`, or `record<{field: T, ...}>`. `list<item>` is SHORTHAND for a list of the app-defined `item` record `{ id: string, name: string, qty?: number, unit?: string, checked?: bool }`, where `id` is a stable, app-assigned entry id (set on insert) that makes cross-skill append/patch merges commutative and deterministic. The pattern accepts the closed forms and rejects arbitrary type strings.

emitEntry.description: A widget event (§9.2 / §9.3 #4). SHORTHAND: a bare event name string → normalized to `{name, to_chat: <widget-type default>}`. CANONICAL: `{name, to_chat}` to override the type default. A `to_chat` event posts a system line into the transcript; posting does NOT auto-run inference (§9.3 #4).

tool.description: A single tool reference with optional per-tool config and explicit state routing (§9.3). `writes_state`/`reads_state` name the slots the tool touches — this explicit declaration, not name-guessing, is the binding. A tool that returns a result but declares no `writes_state` routes it to `updates_widget`, or to chat by default (§9.3).

config.description: Optional tool-specific configuration. Constrained per `ref` for the well-known tools (see conditional subschemas).

reads_state.description: Slots this tool reads as input (§9.3).

writes_state.description: Slots this tool writes; every widget with `binds_state` on such a slot re-renders on write (§9.3).

updates_widget.description: Fallback routing target for a tool result that declares no `writes_state` (§9.3); if absent, the result routes to chat.

domains.description: Unit domains this instance handles (mirrors the Tool Runtime `UnitDomain`).

list_id.description: The shared-state list slot this list-management instance operates on.

slot.description: Slot name; the routing key across skills (§9.3).

access.description: `read` = this skill only reads the slot; `read_write` = it may be the writer-of-record. A slot has exactly one writer-of-record: the highest-priority `read_write` declarer (§8.3.4). Non-writers append/patch (list/record) or cannot set (scalar).

schema.description: Slot type in the closed type language (§8.3.4).

panel.description: A widget/panel declaration (§9.2). One canonical widget contract; the manifest may write SHORTHAND — type-specific props inline (e.g. `checkable: true`) instead of under `props`, and `region`/`priority`/`emits`/`min_widget_version` omitted for per-widget-type defaults (§9.2). Unknown top-level keys and inline props that do not belong to the declared widget type are rejected (`unevaluatedProperties: false`).

type.description: Widget type; must exist in the app's widget library (§9.2 / §9.4).

id.description: Panel id, unique within the agent (§9.2). Collisions across skills dedupe when type+id+binding match (§8.3.3).

region.description: Layout region (§9.5). SHORTHAND: omitted → the widget type's default region.

priority.description: Placement priority within a region (§9.5). SHORTHAND: omitted → declared/inherited default.

props.description: CANONICAL typed props bag for the widget type (§9.2). Shorthand inline props normalize into this object on load.

binds_state.description: Two-way binding to a shared-state slot (§8.3.4 / §9.3 #1). Two-way ONLY if the owning skill is the slot's writer-of-record; otherwise the app binds it read-only (renders live, edit affordances disabled with a tooltip naming the owner). MUST reference a slot the agent declares (certification cross-checks).

binds_tool.description: User actions on this widget invoke this tool deterministically — the UI-first path, no model round-trip (§8.4 / §9.3 #2). MUST reference a tool the skill declares.

emits.description: Events this widget emits (§9.2 / §9.3 #4). SHORTHAND: omitted → the widget type's default event set; a bare string → `{name, to_chat: <type default>}`.

min_widget_version.description: Minimum widget-library version this panel needs (§9.8). SHORTHAND: omitted → `1.0`. An unknown/too-new widget renders a graceful placeholder, never a crash.

multi.description: SHORTHAND: allow many concurrent named timers (§9.4). Normalizes to `props.multi`.

checkable.description: SHORTHAND: items can be checked off (§9.4). Normalizes to `props.checkable`.

reorderable.description: SHORTHAND: items can be reordered. Normalizes to `props.reorderable`.

max_items.description: SHORTHAND: cap on item count. Normalizes to `props.max_items`.

options.description: SHORTHAND: the mutually-exclusive option labels (e.g. `["US", "Metric"]`). Normalizes to `props.options`.

default.description: SHORTHAND: initially-selected option (must be one of `options`). Normalizes to `props.default`.

actions.description: SHORTHAND action buttons; each triggers a declared tool or inserts a prompt (§9.4). Normalizes to `props.actions`.

mode.description: SHORTHAND determinate vs indeterminate (§9.4). Normalizes to `props.mode`.

min.description: SHORTHAND earliest selectable ISO date/time. Normalizes to `props.min`.

max.description: SHORTHAND latest selectable ISO date/time. Normalizes to `props.max`.

image.description: SHORTHAND reference to a sanitized-SVG asset for the card (§9.1/§9.4). Normalizes to `props.image`.

tab_ids.description: SHORTHAND ids of the panels grouped into this tabbed container (§9.4/§9.5). Normalizes to `props.tab_ids`.
